description_long: This is a large, ornate house with a steeply pitched, multi-gabled roof covered in reddish-brown tiles, and numerous dormer windows. The exterior walls are finished in a light, off-white stone or stucco, giving the building a stately and historic appearance. The structure features several prominent chimneys, decorative stonework, and a variety of window shapes and sizes, including tall rectangular windows and smaller attic windows. The front facade is distinguished by a grand arched entryway, flanked by columns and topped with a small balcony, as well as manicured hedges and a gravel driveway. The grounds include neatly trimmed lawns and pathways, with some areas bordered by low stone walls. The overall design suggests a blend of French Chateau and Gothic Revival architectural influences, with an emphasis on verticality and elaborate detailing.
description: A grand, historic house with steep gabled roofs, ornate stonework, and a prominent arched entryway, surrounded by manicured lawns and hedges.
description_view_0: The front view shows the main arched entryway, flanked by columns and topped with a balcony, as well as the symmetrical arrangement of gables and dormers.
description_view_1: This side view highlights the length of the house, the variety of window sizes, and the extensive roofline with multiple chimneys and gables.
description_view_2: The rear three-quarter view reveals the back and side facades, including additional gables, a smaller outbuilding, and more of the landscaped grounds.
description_view_3: The rear view displays a simpler facade with fewer windows, a long hedge, and the continuation of the steeply pitched roof.
attribute_window_arrangement: asymmetrical placement with a mix of tall rectangular and smaller dormer windows
attribute_entryway_design: Grand arched entryway with columns, balcony above, and decorative stonework, accessed by a gravel driveway and flanked by manicured hedges.
